Inspection Item: External hull condition: corrosion, pitting, coating breakdown, dents, deformation
Draft marks, load line marks, Plimsoll marks visible and painted
Deck plating: cracks, wastage, coating, non-slip surface condition
Hatch covers, deck penetrations, and weathertight doors tightness
Ballast tank access hatches sealed and maintained
Mooring winches, brakes, and rendering markers in good order. Brake thickness is adequate
Mooring ropes/wires in good condition, certificates available.
Anchors, windlass, chain cable condition and bitter end securing
Hydraulic systems: leaks, hoses, oil levels, guards in place
Cargo manifold pressure gauges, drip trays, reducers condition
Deck cranes/davit condition and load test records
Cargo pumps (main, stripping, emergency) operational
Cargo lines and valves free from leaks, pressure test done prior last discharge, manifold pressure transmitter working in good condition
Cargo tank access lids, gaskets, ladders condition
Tank atmosphere monitoring system calibrated
Inert Gas System: blower operation, oxygen content, deck water seal
Crude Oil Washing system - equipment readiness, lines flushed, safety interlocks working / no alarms on panel
Vapor return line condition / sensors working in good order
Ballast pumps operational, valves responsive
Ballast tanks coating, internal condition, anodes status
Remote sounding, gauging systems operational
Ballast Water Management System functional, logs updated
Main engine: condition, alarms, safety devices, leaks. Any issues noted in last performance analysis. MECYL dosing is optimised
Auxiliary engines: running condition, vibration, exhaust leaks. Any concerns in last performance analysis.
Boilers and economizers: safety valves not leaking, alarms functional. Boiler water test results to be reviewed and if possible witnessed
Steering gear: emergency and local operation tested / last test date for a short visit
Fuel and lube oil purifiers operational. All panels in good order. No alarms on panels
Bilge system: alarms and pumps tested, free from leaks
Oily Water Separator and 15 ppm monitor tested
Quick-closing valves and remote stops tested
Main switchboard clean, no overheating signs, insulation test records
Emergency generator auto-start tested
Lighting throughout vessel (navigation, deck, engine, accommodation)
UPS and emergency battery backup functional. Battery ready reckoner to be reviewed
Fire detection/alarm panel working. Date is correct and no alarms on panel
Fire extinguishers: in place, serviced, tagged
Fire pumps and emergency fire pump tested
CO2 fixed system: cylinders secured, alarms functional
Foam monitors, hydrants, hoses condition
Fire detection loops tested
Emergency shutdown systems tested
Lifeboats: engines run, release gears tested, equipment complete
Liferaft: hydrostatic release units valid
PPE: BA sets functional, spare cylinders charged, EEBDs in place
Compasses: magnetic and gyro checked
Navigation equipment: radar, ECDIS, GPS, AIS, VDR operational
Bridge alarms: BNWAS, steering gear alarms tested
GMDSS: radio tests, EPIRB, SARTs, Navtex, batteries checked
Passage planning, charts, and publications updated
ODME system tested and records available
Oil record books Part I & II maintained correctly
Slop tank - fixed Wilden pump and dump valve arrangements operational
Garbage segregation and Garbage Record Book up to date
Sewage treatment plant functional
SOPEP kit complete and easily accessible
Accommodation block structural integrity (corrosion, cracks, paint)
External stairways, handrails, and doors secure
Fire doors operational and self-closing
Emergency lighting in corridors and stairways tested
Ventilation and air-conditioning units working properly
Mess room, galley, provisions store: cleanliness, pest control, food hygiene
Refrigeration rooms: temperature control, door safety release operational
Laundry, sanitary facilities in working condition
Cabins: clean, furniture secure, no unsafe modifications
Medical locker properly stocked and log maintained
Accommodation gas detection systems tested
Muster list posted and updated
Emergency communication systems (CCR-Bridge-Engine-Accommodation)
Abandon ship and fire drills records checked. If sailing inspection is performed conduct a drill for checking crew response and emergency equipment readiness
Emergency escape routes marked, free of obstruction
Check the DD specs / jobs assigned to DD
Check the UPLM / Pending PMS jobs / critical jobs overdue. Check randomly the inventory and confirm all critical spares are onboard.

Check the knowledge and competence of crew members. Make comments for performance exceeding the requirements. Master and CE intermediate or signoff scope to be made for the onboard performance evaluation
Check the vessel budget status, planned spares over next 3 months.
Check the ongoing projects of the year and the progress of the same. Check EMOC is up to date and confirm the status of all open EMOC.
